pattern: Running prefix and suffix sums let every split point be evaluated in constant time, so one left-to-right sweep finds the index that minimises the floored-average gap.
hints: Recomputing both averages from scratch at every index is wasteful; the prefix portion grows by one element each step.
Track the total sum once, then maintain a running prefix sum so the suffix sum is just total minus prefix.

For each i compute floor(prefix/(i+1)) and floor(suffix/(n-i-1)) (treating an empty suffix average as 0), take the absolute difference, and remember the smallest, breaking ties toward the lower index.

edge_inputs.description: all equal so many indices tie
best split is at the last index where suffix average is 0

stress.description: large random array exercising prefix/suffix accumulation
stress.note: O(n^2) recomputation would time out at this size

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def minimumAverageDifference(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        best_idx = 0
        best_val = None
        for i in range(n):
            left = nums[:i + 1]
            right = nums[i + 1:]
            la = sum(left) // len(left)
            ra = sum(right) // len(right) if right else 0
            d = abs(la - ra)
            if best_val is None or d < best_val:
                best_val = d
                best_idx = i
        return best_idx

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def minimumAverageDifference(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        total = sum(nums)
        prefix = 0
        best_idx = 0
        best_val = None
        for i in range(n):
            prefix += nums[i]
            la = prefix // (i + 1)
            rem = n - i - 1
            ra = (total - prefix) // rem if rem else 0
            d = abs(la - ra)
            if best_val is None or d < best_val:
                best_val = d
                best_idx = i
        return best_idx
